creator: exploringlondon
caption:
ad Step inside the new exhibition, The Last Princesses of Punjab, and discover the lives of five remarkable women, including Sophia Duleep Singh, the goddaughter of Queen Victoria. Raised in royal circles, Sophia chose to fight for something bigger, becoming a leading voice in the movement for women’s rights. Now, her story and those of the women around her are being brought to life through powerful objects, personal stories, and rarely seen artefacts.

🎟️ Use code EXPLORING20 for 20% off tickets + student savings available (link to tickets in bio and stories!)
🗓️ Open until 8 Nov 2026
📍 Kensington Palace, Kensington Gardens, London, W8 4PX

transcription.text: You might recognize this palace in London, but did you know that it holds a story that you've not heard before? This is of course Kensington Palace, and they have an incredible new exhibition called The Last Princesses of Punjab, where you'll discover the story of Sophia Dilip Singh, her mother, her grandmother, and her sisters. And you might not have heard of Princess Sophia, but she was actually the goddaughter of Queen Victoria. And despite that privilege, she and her two sisters did things differently. They fought for women's rights, and they challenged societal norms. The exhibition is so moving, you'll learn about how they campaigned for women's right to vote, how they nursed and advocated for soldiers, as well as providing shelter to refugees. It is honestly so fascinating. So definitely go and discover The Last Princesses of Punjab, and use our code EXPLORING20 for 20% off your tickets. Find all the details in the caption.
